mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the cost of capital and increase the present value of future corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9% indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses wage income and consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated unemployment with a simultaneously low federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary accommodation aimed at countering labor market weakness, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal instances produce positive 20-day returns less than 52% of the time, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth predicts positive near-term S&P 500 returns exclusively during post-recession recovery phases when inventory restocking and capacity utilization rebound amplify earnings surprises.
failure_condition: During post-recession quarters, month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts signals strengthening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad-based demand expansion without supply bottlenecks, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint upward moves in INDPRO and RSAFS are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises short-term borrowing costs and reduces the relative attractiveness of equities versus cash, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Instances of month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a tightening labor market that boosts consumer income expectations and aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signals of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of observations.
